description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who needs to hand off a small,
    cleaned sample of training data to a colleague.

    Premises already present in the workspace:
      • The directory  /home/user/ml_data/        (permissions 755)  
      • The SQLite database file  /home/user/ml_data/training.db
        containing a single table named  training_samples
        with the exact schema and rows shown below.

    Schema of table  training_samples
        id    INTEGER  PRIMARY KEY
        text  TEXT
        label INTEGER

    Current rows
        1 | hello world   | 0
        2 | goodbye world | 1
        3 | test sample   | 0

    Task
      1. Export the entire contents of the table  training_samples
         to a CSV file located at
             /home/user/ml_data/export/training_samples.csv
         (create the  export  sub-directory if it does not yet exist).

    Strict CSV formatting requirements that the automated grader
    will verify:
      • Comma-separated values, no spaces before or after commas.
      • The very first line must be the header line
            id,text,label
      • The data rows must appear in ascending id order,
        exactly matching the order shown above.
      • Each line, including the last one, must end with a single
        UNIX newline character “\n”.
      • No extra blank lines are allowed anywhere in the file.

      Therefore the final file
        /home/user/ml_data/export/training_samples.csv
      must contain exactly the 4 lines below:

        id,text,label
        1,hello world,0
        2,goodbye world,1
        3,test sample,0

    You may accomplish the export however you wish, but it can be done
    with a single shell command.  Do not hard-code paths other than the
    ones given above.

    When you have finished, the grader will look only for:
      • The presence of /home/user/ml_data/export/training_samples.csv
      • The file’s contents matching the exact 4-line CSV specified.

          (1,'hello world',0),
          (2,'goodbye world',1),
          (3,'test sample',0);
